title: reseda alba subsp. maritima
intro: Reseda alba subsp. maritima, also known as sea mignonette, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the coastal areas of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Reseda alba subsp. maritima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to support digestive health and alleviate various health issues.
